name: Donald Trump
summary: Donald Trump's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a history of discriminatory actions, financial recklessness, and disregard for the law. He has been involved in numerous bankruptcies, defaults, and lawsuits, including those related to his business dealings and allegations of fraud and defamation. Trump has also demonstrated a willingness to use racist and sexist language, and has implemented policies that target marginalized groups, including immigrants, Muslims, and LGBTQ individuals. His actions have been characterized by a lack of accountability, a tendency to blame others, and a willingness to use his power to silence critics and opponents. Trump's behavior has been marked by a consistent disregard for the truth, and he has been found liable for sexual abuse, defamation, and business fraud, and has been convicted of falsifying business records.

entries.fact: Donald Trump countersued the U.S. government for $100 million over charges that his properties had discriminated against Black applicants and tenants, with the help of Roy Cohn.
Donald Trump filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection for six of his businesses, including the Plaza Hotel in Manhattan and the casinos in Atlantic City, New Jersey.
Trump filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection for the Trump Taj Mahal casino, financed with $675 million in junk bonds and completed for $1.1 billion, and personally guaranteed future performance.
The Plaza Hotel, owned by Donald Trump, filed for bankruptcy protection, and a reorganization plan was approved a month later, with the banks taking control of the property.
Trump's Castle and Plaza casinos filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.

Donald Trump defaulted on over $3 billion of bank loans, and the lenders seized the Plaza Hotel along with most of his other properties in a 'vast and humiliating restructuring' that allowed him to avoid personal bankruptcy.
Trump sold the Plaza Hotel to Saudi and Asian investors.
Trump Plaza Casino's stock began trading on the Big Board.
Trump Hotels & Casino Resorts, which owned the Trump Plaza, Taj Mahal, and Trump Castle, went bankrupt.

Trump declared the Trump International Hotel and Tower in Chicago to be worthless on his tax return, despite writing off losses incurred through construction cost overruns and lagging sales of residential units.
Trump Hotels & Casino Resorts went bankrupt again, leaving Trump with 10% ownership.
The State of New York filed a $40 million civil suit against Trump University, alleging that the company made false statements and defrauded consumers.
NBC and Univision dropped the Miss Universe pageants in reaction to Trump's comments about Mexican immigrants.
Donald Trump announced his candidacy for the 2016 presidential election, campaigning as a rich, successful businessman and an outsider without political experience.
Donald Trump claimed Mexican immigrants were 'bringing drugs, they're bringing crime, they're rapists' in his campaign launch speech.
The New York attorney general's office ordered the Trump Foundation to cease its fundraising activities in New York immediately due to violating state law.
The New York attorney general's office filed a civil suit against the Trump Foundation, Trump, and his adult children, seeking $2.8 million in restitution and additional penalties.
Trump was impeached for abuse of power and obstruction of Congress.
Trump declared Mar-a-Lago as his primary residence.
A New York state judge ordered Trump to pay $2 million to a group of charities for misusing the Trump Foundation's funds, in part to finance his presidential campaign.
Trump downplayed the severity of the COVID-19 pandemic, contradicted health officials, and signed the CARES Act in response to the pandemic.
Trump was impeached for incitement of insurrection.
Trump was found liable in civil cases for sexual abuse and defamation and for business fraud.
Trump was found guilty in 34 counts of falsifying business records, making him the first U.S. president convicted of a felony.
Trump imposed tariffs on nearly all countries at the highest level since the Great Depression.
Trump initiated mass layoffs of federal workers at the beginning of his second presidency.
Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.
Trump's administration targeted political opponents and civil society, persecuted transgender people, and conducted mass deportation of immigrants.
Trump was sentenced to a discharge.
Trump authorized a joint U.S./Israeli war with Iran that resulted in the killing of Iran's leader.
Donald Trump made 'explicitly racist and sexist appeals to win over white voters' during his 2016 presidential campaign.
Donald Trump described NATO as 'obsolete'.
The FBI launched an investigation into possible links between Russia and Trump's 2016 campaign, known as Crossfire Hurricane.
Trump reduced the budget for renewable energy research by 40 percent and reversed Obama-era policies directed at curbing climate change
Trump withdrew from the Paris Agreement, making the U.S. the only nation to not ratify it
Trump rolled back more than 100 federal environmental regulations, including those that curbed greenhouse gas emissions, air and water pollution, and the use of toxic substances

Trump dismantled federal regulations on health, labor, the environment, and other areas, including a bill that revoked the Obama-era regulation restricting the sale of firearms to severely mentally ill people.
Trump delayed, suspended, or reversed ninety federal regulations, often after requests by the regulated industries.
Trump described illegal immigration as an 'invasion' of the United States and drastically escalated immigration enforcement.
Trump implemented a travel ban targeting predominantly Muslim countries, which was blocked by courts but later upheld by the Supreme Court in 2019.
Trump signed an executive order to scale back the implementation of the Affordable Care Act.
Trump signed an executive order that denied entry to citizens from six Muslim-majority countries for four months and from Syria indefinitely.
Trump replaced the initial travel ban with a presidential proclamation extending travel bans to North Koreans, Chadians, and some Venezuelan officials.
The Supreme Court allowed the revised travel ban to go into effect.
Trump signed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017, which reduced tax rates for businesses and individuals and eliminated the penalty associated with the Affordable Care Act's individual mandate
Trump signed legislation to increase funding for drug treatments in response to the opioid epidemic, but was widely criticized for failing to make a concrete strategy.
Trump barred organizations that provide abortions or abortion referrals from receiving federal funds.
The Trump administration had a policy of family separation that separated over 4,400 children from migrant parents at the U.S.-Mexico border.
Trump mandated that illegal immigrant families be detained together unless there is a concern of risk for the child, after initially blaming Democrats for the family separation policy.
A federal judge ordered that the families be reunited and further separations stopped except in limited circumstances.
Trump began a trade war with China by imposing tariffs and other trade barriers.
Trump's refusal to sign any spending bill unless it allocated funding for the border wall resulted in the longest-ever federal government shutdown, for 35 days from December 2018 to January 2019.
The federal budget deficit increased by almost 50 percent, to nearly $1 trillion, under Trump
The U.S. national debt increased by 39 percent, reaching $27.75 trillion, and the U.S. debt-to-GDP ratio hit a post-World War II high under Trump
Trump's administration took an anti-marijuana position, revoking Obama-era policies that provided protections for states that legalized marijuana.
Trump oversaw the federal government execute 13 prisoners, more than in the previous 56 years combined, ending a 17-year moratorium.
Trump's administration rolled back key components of the Obama administration's workplace protections against discrimination of LGBTQ people.
Trump resigned from SAG-AFTRA to avoid a disciplinary hearing regarding the January 6 attack, and two days later, the union permanently barred him.
Trump granted 73 pardons and commuted 70 sentences in his last full day in office, including pardons for people with personal or political connections to him, his family, and his allies.
Trump dismissed Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) director James Comey, saying a few days later that he was concerned about Comey's role in the Trump-Russia investigations.
Trump disparaged courts and judges he disagreed with, often in personal terms, and questioned the judiciary's constitutional authority.
The Supreme Court upheld the revised travel ban.
Mueller's final report found that Russia interfered in 2016 to favor Trump and that Trump and his campaign welcomed and encouraged the effort.
Trump pressured Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy to investigate Joe and Hunter Biden, in an attempt to gain an advantage in the 2020 presidential election.
The U.S. withdrew from the Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty, citing alleged Russian noncompliance.
Journalist E. Jean Carroll sued Trump for defamation over his denial of raping her in the 1990s.
The House voted to impeach Trump for abuse of power and obstruction of Congress.
Trump established the White House Coronavirus Task Force in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The Senate acquitted Trump of the impeachment charges.
Trump halted funding of the World Health Organization in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Trump hosted the signing of the Abraham Accords between Israel and the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain to normalize their foreign relations.
Trump sowed doubts about the election, claiming without evidence that it would be rigged and that widespread use of mail balloting would produce massive election fraud.
Trump declared victory before the results were known on the morning after the election.
Trump baselessly alleged election fraud after Joe Biden was projected the winner.
Trump and his allies filed many lawsuits challenging the election results, which were rejected by at least 86 judges in both state and federal courts for having no factual or legal basis.
Trump held a rally at which he called for the election to be overturned and urged his supporters to 'fight like hell' and 'take back our country' by marching to the Capitol.
The House charged Trump with incitement of insurrection for his role in the January 6 Capitol attack.
The Senate acquitted Trump of the impeachment charges for his role in the January 6 Capitol attack.
Trump began using the term 'big lie' to refer to the 2020 election itself, after initially having his false claims concerning the election referred to as the 'big lie' by his critics.
Trump took credit for the overturning of Roe v. Wade by the Supreme Court in the case of Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization, after appointing three anti-abortion justices to the Court.
FBI agents searched Mar-a-Lago for illegally held documents, including those in breach of the Espionage Act, collecting 11 sets of classified documents, some marked top secret.
New York filed a civil lawsuit against Trump accusing him of inflating the Trump Organization's value to gain an advantage with lenders and banks.
Trump was found liable for sexual abuse and defamation and ordered to pay $5 million in one case and $83.3 million in another.

The U.S. House committee on the January 6 attack recommended criminal charges against Trump for obstructing an official proceeding, inciting or assisting an insurrection, and conspiracy to defraud the United States.
A federal grand jury constituted by Special Counsel Jack Smith indicted Trump on 31 counts of 'willfully retaining national defense information' under the Espionage Act, among other charges.
A grand jury in Fulton County, Georgia, indicted Trump on 13 charges, including racketeering, for his efforts to subvert the 2020 election in the state.
Trump's campaign began diverting 10% of donations to his leadership PAC, and had paid $100 million towards his legal bills by March 2024.

Trump was convicted on 34 felony counts of falsifying business records, stemming from evidence that he booked Michael Cohen's hush-money payments to adult film actress Stormy Daniels as business expenses to cover up his alleged 2006-2007 affair with Daniels during the 2016 election.
The judge gave Trump a no-penalty sentence known as an unconditional discharge, saying that punitive requirements would have interfered with presidential immunity.
Donald Trump bragged about kissing and groping women without their consent in a 2005 recording, saying 'when you're a star, they let you do it. You can do anything. ... Grab 'em by the pussy'
Trump's politics and rhetoric led to the formation of a political movement known as Trumpism, which has been compared to a cult of personality.
Trump made increasingly violent and authoritarian statements during his campaign, including saying he would weaponize the FBI and Justice Department against his opponents.
Trump used harsher and more dehumanizing anti-immigrant rhetoric than during his presidency, calling his opponents 'the enemy', 'vermin', and 'fascists'.
Trump ordered agencies to stop enforcement of disfavored rules in an attempt at large-scale deregulation that legal experts described as illegal and contrary to decades of federal law.

Trump sought greater government control over private businesses and shifted away from traditional conservative free market orthodoxy, engaging in state capitalism by taking direct government equity stakes in multiple U.S. corporations.

Trump canceled and paused federal grants and made large cuts to scientific research, several of which were found by judges and the Government Accountability Office as being illegal and unconstitutional.
Trump and his administration's Make America Healthy Again agenda promoted anti-science and anti-vaccine activism, resulting in a resurgence of whooping cough and measles.
Trump relied on declaring national emergencies to justify hundreds of actions and bypass congressional approval or regulatory review.
Trump declared a national energy emergency, allowing the suspension of environmental regulations, loosening the rules for fossil fuel extraction and limiting renewable energy projects.

Trump withdrew the U.S. from the Paris Agreement on climate change during his first presidency, Biden reentered the agreement in 2021, and Trump again withdrew from the agreement during his second presidency.
Trump attributed societal problems to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives and wokeness, equating diversity with incompetence.
Trump reversed pro-diversity policies in the federal government.
Trump threatened cultural institutions and sixty universities on DEI and antisemitism grounds.
Trump forced law firms to capitulate to his political agenda.
Trump became the oldest person to assume the presidency and the first president with a felony conviction.
Trump issued more executive orders than any other U.S. president's first 100 days, with over 550 lawsuits challenging his orders and actions as of mid-January 2026.
Trump implemented a hiring freeze across the federal government and ordered telework of federal employees to be discontinued within 30 days.
Trump ordered a review of many career civil service positions with the intention of reclassifying them into at-will positions without job protections.
Trump initiated mass job terminations of federal employees, which were described by legal experts as unprecedented or in violation of federal law.

Trump ordered an end to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) projects in the federal government and rescinded Lyndon B. Johnson's 1965 Executive Order 11246, which had mandated that federal contractors take affirmative action to end racial discrimination.

Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency largely dismantled several federal agencies, including USAID and the Department of Education, unilaterally fired several thousand staff, and reduced administrative functions to statutory minimums.
Trump threatened, signed executive actions, and ordered investigations into his political opponents, critics, and organizations aligned with the Democratic Party.

Trump's actions against civil society were described by hundreds of legal experts and political scientists as authoritarian, contributing to democratic backsliding, and negatively impacting the rule of law.

Trump granted clemency to all January 6 rioters convicted or charged on his first day in office, including those who violently attacked police, by pardoning more than 1,500 and commuting the sentences of 14.
Trump defied judges and the courts in roughly one third of all cases against him, actions which were described by legal experts as unprecedented for any presidential administration.
Donald Trump posed as a Trump Organization official named 'John Barron' and called journalist Jonathan Greenberg to get a higher ranking on the Forbes 400 list of wealthy Americans.
Donald Trump divorced his first wife Ivana Zelníčková after having an affair with model and actress Marla Maples.
Trump's comments and actions have been characterized as racist, including his explicit and routine disparagement of racial, religious, and ethnic minorities.

Trump condemned the 2017 Unite the Right rally, stating that there were 'very fine people on both sides', implying a moral equivalence between the white supremacist demonstrators and the counter-protesters.
Trump reportedly referred to El Salvador, Haiti, Honduras, and African nations as 'shithole countries', remarks condemned as racist.
A House of Representatives resolution condemned Trump for racist remarks about four minority Democratic congresswomen.
Federal law-enforcement officials used tear gas and other crowd control tactics to remove a largely peaceful crowd of lawful protesters from Lafayette Square, outside the White House.
Trump posed with a Bible for a photo op at the nearby St. John's Episcopal Church, following the forced removal of protesters at Lafayette Square.
Trump's 2024 campaign made extensive use of dehumanizing language and racial stereotypes.
Trump expanded the domestic use of the military and ordered military deployments to several Democratic Party-led cities.

Trump banned transgender people from the military, restricted or defunded gender-affirming healthcare, opposed inclusive language, censored research and education materials, targeted schools, universities, and cultural institutions accused of promoting what his government calls 'gender ideology', barred transgender athletes from sports, and required U.S. passports to state transgender people's sex assigned at birth.
Trump instructed Border Patrol agents to summarily deport illegal immigrants crossing the border and disabled the CBP One app that was being used to schedule border crossings.
Trump resumed the Remain in Mexico policy, designated drug cartels as terrorist groups, and ordered construction to be resumed on a border wall.
Trump revoked the parole status of immigrants who entered the U.S. under CBP One and ended humanitarian parole for Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans.
Trump targeted activists, legal immigrants, tourists, and students with visas who expressed criticism of his policies or pro-Palestinian advocacy.
Several American citizens were detained and deported.
Trump imposed tariffs on most countries, including large tariffs on major trading partners China, Canada, and Mexico.
Trump frequently threatened and enacted tariffs against treaty allies for opposing his political objectives.
Trump selected cabinet members with personal loyalty to him, with the 'focus on loyalty over subject-matter expertise'.
Trump and Vice President Vance berated Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy in a highly contentious televised meeting.
Trump used the Alien Enemies Act of 1798 to imprison illegal immigrants without trial at the Terrorism Confinement Center in El Salvador.
Trump ordered the bombing of Iranian nuclear sites.

Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill Act into law, making the temporary tax cuts of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act permanent and adding additional tax deductions for a total of around $4.5 trillion, mostly benefiting the highest income brackets and costing people in the lowest income bracket $1,600 per year.
Trump's plan for a Gaza ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas was signed, leading to the creation of the Board of Peace.
Trump pardoned former Honduran president Juan Orlando Hernández, who had been extradited to the U.S. in 2022 and sentenced to 45 years in prison for drug trafficking.
Trump threatened the annexation of Greenland, including by military force or the imposition of tariffs on European allies.
The U.S. captured Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro in a military raid in Venezuela and took him to New York, where he was charged with drug trafficking.
Trump's aggressive and hardline deportation campaign led to large nationwide protests and violent confrontations with migrants and protesters.
The Supreme Court ruled the tariffs Trump imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to be illegal.
Trump authorized joint U.S./Israeli air strikes on Iran.
Donald Trump promoted the racist 'birther' conspiracy theory that Barack Obama was not born in the United States.

Donald Trump's campaign released a letter from his longtime personal physician, Harold Bornstein, stating that he would 'be the healthiest individual ever elected to the presidency', which was later revealed to be dictated by Trump himself.
Forbes estimated Donald Trump's net worth at $4.5 billion, based on interviews with more than 80 sources.
Donald Trump made over 30,573 false or misleading statements during his first presidency, according to The Washington Post's fact-checkers.
Donald Trump promoted conspiracy theories such as climate change denial, alleged Ukrainian interference in U.S. elections, and vaccines and autism.
Donald Trump characterized his comments about kissing and groping women without their consent as 'locker-room talk'.
Trump issued an executive order scaling back parts of Obamacare.
Donald Trump issued a travel ban that caused chaos and protests at US airports, affecting immigrants and refugees.
Three of Trump's agents seized his medical records in a raid on his physician's office.
Trump rolled back an Obama regulation making it easier for the mentally ill to get guns.
Trump signed a new travel ban directive, which was later halted by a Hawaii judge.
Trump said he plans to 'let Obamacare fail'.
Trump cut funding for Obamacare enrollment programs by 40% and shortened the enrollment period.
Trump signed a health care executive order that allowed small businesses and associations to band together to buy health insurance plans that might not meet Obamacare's standards.
The American Political Science Association ranked Donald Trump as the worst president.

At least 26 women have publicly accused Donald Trump of sexual misconduct, including rape, kissing without consent, groping, looking under women's skirts, and walking in on naked teenage pageant contestants.
At least 54 criminal cases from August 2015 to April 2020 invoked Donald Trump's name in connection with violence or threats of violence, mostly by white men and primarily against minorities.
Donald Trump's rhetoric has been associated with an increased incidence of hate crimes and has an emboldening effect on expressing prejudicial attitudes.
Trump frequently accused the press of bias, calling it the 'fake news media' and 'the enemy of the people'.
Twitter began to tag some of Trump's tweets with fact-checks.
Donald Trump refused to condemn the white supremacist Proud Boys during a presidential debate and told them to 'stand back and stand by'.
Trump was banned from Twitter after the January 6 attack.
Trump was banned from Facebook and Instagram after the January 6 attack.
Trump repeatedly voiced support for outlawing political dissent and criticism.
Trump said that reporters should be prosecuted for not divulging confidential sources and media companies should possibly lose their broadcast licenses for unfavorable coverage of him.
The American Political Science Association again ranked Donald Trump as the worst president.
Forbes estimated Donald Trump's net worth at $5.1 billion and ranked him the 700th wealthiest person in the world.

Media attention and public pressure mounted on Donald Trump's administration for not releasing files relating to his friend, convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, despite Trump's promise to do so during the 2024 campaign.
Some of the files related to Jeffrey Epstein were released, mostly heavily redacted.
Roughly three million additional pages related to Jeffrey Epstein were released, including approximately 38,000 references to Donald Trump, his wife, Mar-a-Lago, and other Trump-related terms.
The Supreme Court partly reinstated Trump's travel ban, allowing it to go into effect for some individuals.
Trump extended his travel ban to include blocks on North Korea, Venezuela, and Chad.
The US became one of the only countries in the world not to be a part of the Paris climate agreement after Syria announced it would join
The Supreme Court allowed Trump's latest travel ban to go into full effect.
The Supreme Court upheld Trump's travel ban in a 5-4 decision.
Trump signed a bill to temporarily reopen the government after the longest shutdown in history.
Trump declared a national emergency to build a border wall, setting up a massive legal fight.
Trump proposed cuts to climate and clean-energy programs
Trump used words like 'invasion' and 'killer' at rallies more than 500 times since 2017, according to a USA Today analysis.
Trump cut refugee admissions to the US to a record low.
The Senate confirmed Dan Brouillette, a former Ford lobbyist, as energy secretary
Trump said 'don't think science knows' when asked about climate change
Trump refused to commit to a peaceful transition of power, prompting the Pentagon to stress it would play no role in the election.
Trump virtually cut off refugees as he unleashed a tirade on immigrants.
The Trump administration had sabotaged the Affordable Care Act in six major ways
The Trump administration had rolled back over 100 environmental rules
Trump declared victory in the presidential election despite results from key states being unclear.
Trump revived baseless claims of election fraud after Biden won the presidential race.
The Supreme Court rejected a Texas lawsuit seeking to subvert the presidential election.
Trump pressured the Georgia secretary of state to recalculate the vote in his favor during an hour-long call.
A pro-Trump mob assaulted the Capitol, resulting in multiple deaths and injuries.
The Trump administration carried out its 13th and final execution.
Trump urged a Wisconsin legislator to overturn his 2020 defeat in the state.
Agents at Trump's Mar-a-Lago seized 11 sets of classified documents, according to a court filing
New York attorney general filed a civil fraud lawsuit against Trump, some of his children, and his business
E. Jean Carroll, who accused Trump of rape, sued him for defamation
A jury found Trump liable for battery and defamation in E. Jean Carroll trial
Trump was indicted on 37 federal criminal counts in the Mar-a-Lago case
Donald Trump and allies were indicted in Georgia over a bid to reverse the 2020 election loss
An appeals court upheld a $5 million award in a sexual abuse verdict against President-elect Trump
Donald Trump raised birther questions about President Obama
Donald Trump entertained conspiracy theories
A Harvard study criticized the media's role in Donald Trump's rise
Trump fraud trial penalty will exceed $450 million
Trump was convicted on all counts to become America's first felon president
A federal judge dismissed the Trump classified documents case over concerns with the prosecutor's appointment
Trump received an unconditional discharge sentence in the hush money case
Trump signed executive order directing US withdrawal from the Paris climate agreement — again
Trump signs sweeping actions on immigration and border security on Day 1
Federal Workers Ordered to Report on Colleagues Over D.E.I. Crackdown
Oil Companies Embrace Trump, but Not 'Drill, Baby, Drill'
Trump's sweeping immigration crackdown targets some legal means to enter U.S., too
As Trump Attacks Diversity, a Racist Undercurrent Surfaces
Under Trump, conservatives reignite a battle over race and the Constitution
Trump's executive actions curbing transgender rights focus on 'gender ideology'
The Trump administration continued to hollow out the federal workforce, having already fired more than 30,000 employees.
Explainer: What is El Salvador's mega-prison holding Venezuelans deported from the US?
Trump's Science Policies Pose Long-Term Risk, Economists Warn
Suggesting More Troops in More Cities, Trump Bends Military's Role
Federal Judge Deems Trump Administration's Termination of NIH Grants Illegal
Tax Cuts Now, Benefit Cuts Later: The Timeline in the Republican Megabill
What's in the tax and spending bill that Trump has signed into law
Trump Embraces State Capitalism
Trump illegally froze 1,800 NIH medical research grants, Congress' watchdog says
Trump is tightening his grip on the economy, taking a page from China
Trump wants to command bosses like Xi does. He is failing
A divided court eliminated Trump's half-billion-dollar fine in a fraud case
How Trump Used 10 Emergency Declarations to Justify Hundreds of Actions
Trump threatens 'Apocalypse Now'-style action against Chicago to boost deportations
A judge dismissed the Georgia election interference case against Trump
Trump's attacks on the media showed 'ominous signs' for the American press
Trump has a long history with conspiracy theories
Trump escalated rhetoric on outlawing political dissent and criticism
President Trump said he 'brought back free speech,' but his first 100 days told a different story
Trump's moves against the media mirrored approaches by authoritarian leaders to silence dissent
Trump's escalating attacks on vaccines shock public health leaders
Multiple states sued over Trump's new global tariffs imposed after his Supreme Court loss.
Trump pressured Colorado Gov. Polis to release Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted in a scheme to find proof of fraud in the 2020 presidential election.
The DOJ released Epstein files containing sexual assault allegations against Trump, including notes from FBI interviews with a woman who alleges she was assaulted by Trump as a minor.
Trump ruled out talks with Iran absent its 'unconditional surrender' as Israel strikes Lebanon.
President Trump's unfounded claim that Tylenol could be linked to autism in children resulted in a notable drop in orders for the medication among pregnancy-related emergency department visits.
President Trump forcefully vowed to press on with the US military operation in Iran until the Iranian regime surrenders, despite a majority of Americans opposing the action

President Trump vowed to press on with U.S. military operations against Iran, declaring the country would be 'hit very hard,' and considered 'complete destruction and certain death' due to Iran's behavior.
President Trump launched a war against Iran, throwing the region into chaos and stranding tens of thousands of Americans
President Trump falsely stated that the 2015 nuclear deal gave Iran the right to secure nuclear weapons, a claim rated False by PolitiFact.
President Trump chided the Supreme Court, saying it hasn't had the 'guts to do what's right'
President Trump warned that Iran would soon 'be hit very hard' and that the U.S. may expand its target list to 'new areas and groups of people'
